name: Rehoboth Community Development Corporation
description_short: Faith-based nonprofit providing free tax preparation, affordable housing development, financial wellness, and community services to low-to-moderate income families across Phoenix's Canyon Corridor and Maricopa County.
description_long:
Rehoboth Community Development Corporation (RCDC) is a faith-based 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization and ministry of Rehoboth Saints Center, Inc., operating for 19+ years in the Canyon Corridor and greater Maricopa County area. The organization serves a culturally diverse community of over 4,000 international residents speaking 52 languages, with a mission centered on social and economic justice through collaboration and community empowerment.

RCDC offers a comprehensive suite of free and low-cost services including IRS-certified Volunteer Income Tax Assistance (VITA) for low and moderate-income individuals, seniors, disabled persons, and limited-English speakers; the PEACE Program; the TIME Business Program for entrepreneurship; affordable housing development and homeownership initiatives; financial wellness education; and youth services. Their recent $21 million affordable housing project in partnership with Gorman & Company (funded by Arizona Department of Housing, City of Phoenix, and Home Matters) demonstrates significant scale and institutional support.

The organization distinguishes itself through deep community roots, culturally competent service delivery in a multilingual environment, and a focus on the "missing middle"—households earning 80-125% of Area Median Income priced out of both subsidized and market-rate housing. RCDC's approach emphasizes generational impact, stability, and long-term wealth-building through homeownership rather than temporary assistance. Their board governance, published staff directory, and transparent nonprofit status indicate institutional legitimacy.

As a purely nonprofit organization without commercial lending products, RCDC is best suited for individuals seeking free tax preparation, affordable housing pathways, and financial education rather than immediate emergency cash. Their services require navigation to specific programs and may involve application processes or eligibility screening. While their 19-year track record and substantial funding demonstrate credibility, service availability may be geographically limited to the Phoenix Canyon Corridor area and surrounding Maricopa County.
pros: IRS-certified VITA volunteers provide free tax preparation for eligible low-to-moderate income filers, seniors, disabled individuals, and limited-English speakers
Multilingual, culturally competent service delivery in a community with 52 languages spoken and 4,000+ international residents
Significant institutional funding ($21M+ for affordable housing) demonstrates credibility and sustainability
Affordable housing development focused on working families and 'missing middle' income earners (80-125% AMI) with 8-unit Harmony 35 project underway
19-year operational track record with published board members, staff directory, and transparent nonprofit governance
Comprehensive service portfolio spanning tax assistance, financial wellness, business training, youth services, and housing—one-stop community development
Faith-based mission aligned with social justice values emphasizes community collaboration and generational wealth-building
cons: Geographic service area limited to Canyon Corridor and Maricopa County; residents outside this region may not qualify
No immediate emergency financial assistance—all programs appear to require applications, eligibility screening, and scheduled appointments rather than same-day cash
Website lacks specific eligibility income thresholds, wait times, or detailed program eligibility criteria for prospective clients
Housing programs are development-focused (building new units) rather than providing immediate rental or purchase assistance for existing inventory
Limited information on response times, appointment availability, or scalability of VITA and other free services given community demand
best_for: Low-to-moderate income filers and seniors needing free IRS-certified tax preparation (VITA eligible individuals)
Working families and 'missing middle' households seeking affordable homeownership in the Phoenix Canyon Corridor area
Multilingual, immigrant, and international residents needing culturally competent financial wellness and community development services
Entrepreneurs seeking free or low-cost business training and support through the TIME Business Program
diagnosis: RCDC is best for low-to-moderate income Phoenix-area residents seeking free tax preparation, affordable housing pathways, financial education, or entrepreneurship support, with particular strength in serving multilingual and immigrant communities. The main caveat is that RCDC is a development and education nonprofit, not an emergency lender—all services require eligibility screening and scheduling, with housing programs focused on building new units rather than immediate rental/purchase assistance.
services: Volunteer Income Tax Assistance (VITA) Program—free IRS-certified tax preparation for low/moderate-income, seniors, disabled, and limited-English speakers
TIME Business Program—entrepreneurship and business training
Affordable housing development (Harmony 35 8-unit townhome project; 66-unit apartment community in partnership with Gorman & Company)
Rental housing information and leasing coordination (via RentCafe partnership)
